intro: This page presents exploratory superconductivity candidates exported from SC SuperLoop into SCLib.
Candidates are generated with physics-informed heuristics, then filtered through prescreening, bounded DFT checks, mechanism audit, and checker review before public display.
The current release uses a preview standard so that early reviewed candidates can be inspected publicly while the evidence base is still growing.

candidates.mechanism_hypothesis: TiN Tc~5K conventional, hard ceramic SC
candidates.review_summary: This candidate has crossed the DFT-screened threshold. The CHGNet force proxy and QE metallic response are consistent with TiN being a robust conventional-metal benchmark-adjacent candidate. This is not a novelty breakthrough candidate, but it is a credible queue anchor for the exploratory-metal branch.

candidates.mechanism_hypothesis: Nb d-metal analog, large lambda expected
candidates.review_summary: This candidate has crossed the DFT-screened threshold as a computed DFT-supported candidate. The current fixed-cell AlB2-type setup supports a finite-DOS metallic interpretation, which is stronger than the earlier queue-promotion-only status. No Tc claim is justified here. The `vc-relax` follow-up and bounded EOS check do not erase the fixed-cell DFT-screened result, but together they block immediate phonon promotion on the present input deck. The next evidence-bearing step should be a pseudopotential / lattice-parameter audit or alternate verified NbB2 setup, not direct phonon promotion.
